ogTitle: Cramps Bay vs Cranbrook: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cramps Bay and Cranbrook.
heading: Cramps Bay vs Cranbrook — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cramps Bay, Tasmania and Cranbrook, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cranbrook has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Cramps Bay (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cramps Bay or Cranbrook safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cramps Bay has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Cranbrook scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cramps Bay scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Cranbrook scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cramps Bay or Cranbrook?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cranbrook has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
